mentions.note: Excellent rocket-launch and satellite-internet business, but an $800 billion IPO valuation with limited financial disclosure makes it too early to call — wait and see.
Private company IPO expected later 2026; Space Mobile, Redwire, Rocket Lab may be proxy plays; IPO timing uncertain; investors hiding in unprofitable space names anticipating deal.
Elon Musk planning IPO by July 2026 to precede Anthropic deal; valued at $88B in recent secondary; raising capital for space-based data centers; potential mega-IPO supply risk.
Seeking $1.5T valuation for late-2026 IPO (recently $1.25T post-XAI merger). Spectrum deal with EchoStar ($17B+) signals strong cash generation. EchoStar acts as backdoor play.
5.5% of Fundrise Innovation Fund; aerospace/defense beneficiary; held by Fundrise at early valuations; part of diversified venture portfolio.
Only space play for average investor; IPO'd June 2026; skip other space names.

Targeting $75B IPO (largest ever) at $1.75T valuation mid-June; unusual reverse roadshow, Elon allocation control, non-traditional lockups, and XAI cash burn create retail risk if insiders exit post-IPO.
Expected IPO June 2026; could be $75B+ deal; Starlink, satellite broadband, XAI subsidiary, and rocket business; money magnet but risks capital flight from other stocks like Tesla.
IPO planned at $1.75T valuation (could reach $2.5T); expected to suck up huge capital from rest of market when IPO launches.
Summer IPO at $2T+ valuation seeking $75B+ raise (3x largest IPO ever). Massive supply influx threatens market; likely hot pop followed by painful 6-month correction.
Part of model builder layer spending on AI infrastructure (recently IPO'd June 2026; included in AI revolution layer 4).

Upcoming IPO valued at $1.75-2T on $8-18.5B revenue (80x sales) vs. profitable tech peers; risks bubble to $5-6T market cap if underwriters limit float, exceeding Nvidia despite vastly lower profitability; monitor for irrational IPO mania.

IPO June 11-12 at ~$2T valuation will likely join Nasdaq 100 on day 7 under fast-track rules. $1.4T Nasdaq 100 ETF exposure guarantees forced buying and early enthusiasm. Risk: insider lockup expiration could crush stock after pop; early retail buyers may overpay and get burned.

June 12 IPO: strong strategic fundamentals (Starlink growth engine, Anthropic $15B/year data center deal, Cursor acquisition) offset by weak metrics (GAAP losses, $14B cash burn); historically rare to bet against Elon but valuation discipline essential—limit orders at $2T market cap or below.

IPO ~$75B at $1.8T valuation with only 4% float initially; 60% locked 12+ months; complex automated releases expand float gradually through 2027; Cramer's $4 trillion valuation target; expect initial surge then churn.

Cramer enthusiastic about SpaceX and Starlink long-term; believes in space opportunity and Musk's ability to create wealth. Cautions retail investors: use limit orders only at IPO open, not market orders; expect opening price ~$270 (2x offer price); book runners should ban market orders and release lockup sellers to prevent runaway $4T+ valuation.

Bullish long-term for patient, multi-generational investors (projects may take lifetimes). IPO Friday oversubscribed with risk of opening too high then crashing hard (Cerebrus scenario). Avoid market orders at opening; wait for post-IPO weakness. Near-term IPO mechanics fraught despite solid fundamentals.
IPO likely to overprice on retail enthusiasm and market orders; substantial downside crash risk (Cerebrus precedent).
IPO pending but cautious on speculator flip dynamic; concerned at 4x oversubscription level; would prefer 10x oversubscription.

IPO tomorrow at $1.75T valuation; risk of overheating with ~20% retail allocation using market orders and post-IPO index inclusion forcing buying; syndicate must maintain control to prevent runaway spike followed by crash.

Starlink has potential to replace internet service providers globally; reusable rocket business has scarcity value; AI infrastructure rental significant ($1.2B+$920M/month). Valuation $2.6T stretched vs. Morgan Stanley but Musk's vision and multiple buyer cohorts (collectors, team members, momentum traders) keep pushing higher. Buy on thesis that long-term revenue projections justify premium.
Largest IPO in history recently completed; Cramer notes aftermath creates opportunity for sleepy interregnum in market.
Starlink competitive threat forcing telecom consolidation; Cramer expects SpaceX to acquire Tesla; IPO'd 2026-06-12 but no price target given.
Successful IPO management ($135 offer, $161 close); spiked to $225 on light volume post-listing, now back down; hostage to lockup mechanics and insider selling unlock schedule.
